L1.area: Certificate verification and internationalized name processing utilities

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the internationalized domain name decoding logic used during X.509 certificate verification. An off-by-one error in a bounds check allows an attacker to overflow four bytes on the stack via a crafted email address in a certificate's name constraints. This can be triggered during TLS connections when a malicious server presents a crafted certificate to a client, or when a malicious client connects to a server that requests client authentication.

L3.area: Punycode encoding and decoding utilities used in certificate name constraint processing
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the punycode decoding implementation used during X.509 certificate name constraint checking. An off-by-one error in a bounds check when decoding internationalized email addresses allows an attacker to write four bytes past the end of a stack buffer. This can be triggered via a crafted email address in a certificate presented during TLS connection establishment.

ground_truth.reason: An off-by-one error in the punycode decoder's bounds check (using > instead of >=) allows a single unsigned int to be written past the allocated buffer during X.509 certificate name constraint verification, enabling a 4-byte stack buffer overflow.
